metadata.title: CW-ERM: Improving Autonomous Driving Planning with Closed-loop Weighted Empirical Risk Minimization
organizations: Woven Planet United Kingdom Limited, Woven Planet North America, Inc.

sections.title: Main Ideas and Motivation
content: Behavior Cloning (BC) algorithms for training self-driving vehicles via pure Empirical Risk Minimization (ERM) biases policy towards matching open-loop behavior.
We debias the policy network by identifying important samples via a closed-loop evaluator.
Training in three phases:Open-loop identification phaseError set construction and evaluation phaseUpsampling training phase
Error set construction and evaluation phase

sections.title: Our Contributions
content: Motivate and propose a technique that leverages closed-loop evaluation metrics acquired from policy rollouts in a simulator to debias the policy network and reduce distributional differences.
Evaluate CW-ERM on a challenging urban driving dataset in a closed-loop fashion to show significant improvements without requiring complex training methods.
Show an important connection of our method to a family of methods that addresses covariate shift through density ratio estimation.

sections.title: Methodology
content: Stage 1 (Identification Policy):Train policy network via BC in open-loop using ERM.
Stage 2 (Closed-Loop Simulation):Perform rollouts in closed-loop simulator. Collect metrics and identify the error set E.
Stage 3 (Final Policy):Train new policy where scenes belonging to the error set are upweighted by a factor w.

sections.title: References
content: 1. Liu et al. (2021). Just Train Twice: Improving Group Robustness without Training Group Information. ICML.2. Vitelli et al. (2022). Safetynet: Safe planning for real-world self-driving vehicles using machine-learned policies. ICRA.3. Scheel et al. (2022). Urban driver: Learning to drive from real-world demonstrations using policy gradients. CoRL.
